title: Yeatesia viridiflora
taxonomy: Yeatesia viridiflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Yeatesia, which is a small group of plants native to Southeast Asia. Yeatesia viridiflora is a type of succulent plant known for its unique, fleshy leaves and vibrant green flowers. The plant's classification also places it within the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint and lavender. Yeatesia viridiflora is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in its native range.
